behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is about translation quality, not wrapper logic. REAL-INFRA: Real HTTP may be involved, but the core failure boundary is model judgment quality. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: A stubbed translator would always return canned good text and hide real failures (semantic drift, ungrammatical phrasing, literal-but-wrong translations). MULTI-STEP: Only one model decision step, so not Workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss real model output errors because no model call occurs. Integration with deterministic fixtures would miss quality regressions from actual model behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes a single translation decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed multilingual evaluation set through the real NMT API and assert adequacy/fluency thresholds (e.g., bilingual rubric or accepted quality scorer), failing on meaning loss or incoherent grammar.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and returns success to caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears at module handoff (API client -> fallback path -> response composer), not a single function. REAL-INFRA: No real network/OS behavior is required to reproduce status-code branching; an in-process stub returning 503 is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Pure unit tests can miss wiring bugs where fallback is never invoked. LLM-DEP: Correctness does not depend on model output quality, only control flow on error. MULTI-STEP: Not a multi-agentic pipeline. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss DI/router misconfiguration where 503 does not trigger fallback in the assembled service. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is specifically about handling a 503 response, not real network timing/transport faults; Agentic rejected because no assertion depends on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with real fallback translator + SQLite repo and stub primary client to return 503; assert HTTP success to caller plus fallback translation payload and no propagated primary error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior depends on repository/query wiring between fallback module and SQLite data. REAL-INFRA: Local DB/filesystem is sufficient; no OS/network/subprocess boundary beyond normal in-process DB access. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked repo can hide SQL/schema/key-normalization bugs. LLM-DEP: No model output dependency. MULTI-STEP: No agentic sequence. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss wrong SQL, collation, or parameter binding that returns incorrect rows despite correct in-memory logic. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only requires local SQLite lookup correctness, not external process/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite database with known phrase mappings, invoke fallback translator through service/repo stack, and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior crosses algorithm + persistence boundary (distance calculation result must be persisted correctly). REAL-INFRA: Can be validated with local modules and test DB; no real network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Unit test of distance function alone can miss storing wrong value/record. LLM-DEP: Assertions can use deterministic strings; no dependence on model quality. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss bugs where computed distance is correct but persistence layer writes null, wrong field, or wrong request id. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text focuses on deterministic metric computation/storage, not evaluating model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke comparison pipeline with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert computed edit distance value and verify persisted metric row (value + correlation id/timestamp fields) in storage.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously in background.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is in orchestration across response path and background quality-tracking path. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/subprocess/network condition is required to verify preference + async dispatch semantics; in-process async executor/test harness is enough. MOCK-HIDE: Unit tests of chooser logic miss regressions where request blocks on logging or background task never enqueued. LLM-DEP: Does not depend on correctness of model-generated content, only source selection and side-effect scheduling. MULTI-STEP: Not multiple model-governed decisions. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss integration bug where controller awaits logger and increases latency, violating background behavior. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real subprocess/OS primitives; Agentic rejected because assertions are about orchestration, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run assembled service with deterministic primary/fallback outputs; assert response uses primary output immediately and verify async quality-log task eventually writes comparison metric without blocking caller.
